case_name: Motrin removal from groundwater for drinking water production
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible treatment process chains for groundwater contaminated with ibuprofen intended for drinking water supply.

input_data.scenario_background: A small drinking water utility has detected Motrin in a municipal groundwater well influenced by nearby septic discharge and wants a reliable treatment upgrade before the water enters supply. The source water is otherwise relatively clean groundwater with low turbidity and modest organic carbon. The utility prefers established treatment approaches that can be operated by conventional staff with moderate capital and operating budgets.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should achieve strong reduction of ibuprofen and be suitable for compliant drinking water production with stable finished water quality.
secondary_goals: use proven treatment technology for trace organic control

core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_adsorption: adsorb dissolved ibuprofen and other trace organic micropollutants from groundwater
core_unit_functions.post_filtration: retain carbon fines and provide polishing before distribution
core_unit_functions.disinfection: ensure microbiological safety of finished drinking water

applicability_rationale: ibuprofen is a trace organic contaminant for which activated carbon is a well-established control approach in drinking water practice
the groundwater has low turbidity and low DOC, which supports efficient adsorption capacity and simpler operation
the utility prefers conventional and robust treatment that can be integrated without high-energy equipment
GAC provides a clear and proven fit for single-contaminant micropollutant polishing in municipal systems
potential_risks: carbon breakthrough requires monitoring and timely media replacement or reactivation
unexpected increases in DOC or competing organics can shorten bed life
biological growth in carbon beds must be managed through appropriate operation and disinfection strategy

core_unit_functions.powdered_activated_carbon_addition: provide adsorption of ibuprofen in a dosed contact step
core_unit_functions.coagulation: capture PAC and associated solids for downstream removal

applicability_rationale: PAC is a recognized treatment option for episodic or moderate trace organic removal in drinking water systems
this route can be feasible where the utility already has or can support basic chemical feed and filtration
it offers flexibility if ibuprofen concentrations vary over time
the approach remains more conventional than advanced membrane systems
potential_risks: adsorption performance depends on contact time, dose control, and competing natural organic matter
continuous chemical handling and solids management increase operator attention
PAC carryover can occur if filtration performance is poor

core_unit_functions.ozonation: oxidize ibuprofen and transform it into more biodegradable intermediates
core_unit_functions.biologically_active_filtration: remove oxidation byproducts and biodegradable dissolved organic matter

applicability_rationale: ozonation is effective for many pharmaceutical micropollutants including ibuprofen
biologically active filtration is commonly paired with ozone to polish oxidation products
the train is technically strong for trace organics but more operationally involved than carbon adsorption alone
it can be considered where the utility is willing to accept moderate process sophistication
potential_risks: oxidation can form transformation products that require downstream biological polishing
bromate formation risk must be assessed if bromide is present even at modest levels
ozone system maintenance and process control are more demanding than simple adsorption systems

core_unit_functions.uv_advanced_oxidation: generate hydroxyl radicals to oxidize ibuprofen and related trace organics
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_adsorption: polish residual parent compound and oxidation byproducts

applicability_rationale: advanced oxidation can degrade pharmaceutical contaminants when a high level of treatment assurance is desired
GAC polishing complements oxidation by capturing residuals and byproducts
the combined train is technically effective but more complex than needed for a straightforward single-contaminant groundwater case
it is better suited as a secondary option when simpler adsorption routes are insufficient
potential_risks: chemical dosing and UV system performance must be tightly controlled
oxidation byproducts may still require polishing and monitoring
higher power demand and greater instrumentation burden reduce fit for conventional low-complexity operation

core_unit_functions.reverse_osmosis: reject dissolved ibuprofen and a broad range of other dissolved contaminants
core_unit_functions.stabilization: adjust permeate chemistry for corrosion control and distribution stability
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide microbiological protection in finished water

applicability_rationale: reverse osmosis is technically capable of removing ibuprofen from groundwater
it may be considered when multiple dissolved contaminants require simultaneous control
for a single trace pharmaceutical in otherwise good-quality groundwater, this is more treatment than necessary
the process ranks lower because it conflicts with the stated preference for moderate cost and conventional operation
potential_risks: concentrate management is a major burden for inland groundwater systems
membrane fouling and scaling increase O&M complexity
post-treatment stabilization is required because permeate can be chemically aggressive
